title: Schema: wizard_progress_tier column + backfill from existing answers
description: Migration 027 adds wizard_progress_tier INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 to player_tennis_preferences and backfills it from existing data so anyone who already filled the form during Sprint 016 testing keeps their progress. Backfill is cascading: wizard_progress_tier = the highest tier N for which any tier-N field is non-null (or any tier-N row exists in player_preferred_partners for tier 4). Tiers are defined in WI-109 — this work item lands the column and the SQL backfill expression. Model + repository updated to read/write the new column; the repo gains a BumpWizardProgressTier(playerID, tier) method that does MAX(current, submitted) so progress never decrements.
acceptance_criteria: Migration 027 adds wizard_progress_tier INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 to player_tennis_preferences
Migration 027 backfills the new column using cascading logic: any non-null tier-N field promotes the row to at least tier N
Tier-to-field mapping in the backfill SQL matches the canonical mapping from WI-109 exactly
Down migration drops the column cleanly without touching other data
Model PlayerTennisPreferences gains WizardProgressTier int field
Repository gains BumpWizardProgressTier(ctx, playerID, tier) using MAX(wizard_progress_tier, ?) — never decrements
Repository GetTennisSummary continues to expose all fields to the admin partial unchanged
Migration applies cleanly on a fresh DB and on a populated DB seeded with partial Sprint 016 answers

files_to_modify: internal/models/models.go — add WizardProgressTier int to PlayerTennisPreferences
internal/repository/player_tennis_preferences.go (or equivalent) — add BumpWizardProgressTier; include new column in scans/inserts

tier_4_partners_and_pressure: any row in player_preferred_partners for this player_id

key_decisions: wizard_progress_tier is monotonic — POST handlers MAX it up, never reset. A user re-editing tier 2 after completing tier 4 stays at tier 4.

Backfill is cascading, not exact — if tier 4 has data but tier 2 doesn't, the user is still considered 'past tier 4' for gating purposes. They simply skipped tiers, which is fine under the optional-everything model.
0 means no submissions at all — distinguishes 'never started' from 'started and skipped tier 1'.

testing_requirements: Migration applies cleanly on fresh DB
Migration applies cleanly on a DB pre-seeded with partial Sprint 016 answers and produces correct cascading wizard_progress_tier values
Down migration removes the column and is reversible
BumpWizardProgressTier never decrements (unit test: bump from 4 with value 2 stays 4)

context.user_story: As the system, I need a single integer per player capturing how far through the wizard they've progressed, so the player-facing UI can show completion state and resume position without ever needing the server to send stored answers back.
design_principles: Privacy preserved — only an integer ever crosses the wire to the player; answers stay admin-only as in Sprint 016
No regressions for existing testers — backfill respects everything they've already shared
